text:
El Niño has officially begun, and it is forecast to intensify into a very strong or "Super" El Niño with major shifts in global weather patterns and an even hotter climate, according to a new report released Thursday morning from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

El Niño is a periodic weather pattern in the tropical Pacific Ocean that alters winds and features unusually hot waters in the central and eastern Pacific. These changes in winds and ocean temperatures have knock-on effects on weather patterns worldwide.

Download the CNN Weather app on IOS for in-depth analysis.

authorMeta.name: plumwildweatherwx
text:
Scattered severe thunderstorms are expected across parts of the central and southern Plains into the Missouri Valley.

The primary threats will be damaging wind gusts, large hail, and a couple of tornadoes. Storms are expected to develop and intensify throughout the afternoon and evening as a strong, unstable air mass combines with favorable wind shear.

Some storms may become supercells capable of producing large hail and localized damaging winds. As storms grow into larger clusters, the threat for widespread damaging winds will increase, with some gusts potentially exceeding 75 mph.

While damaging winds appear to be the main concern, embedded supercells could also produce a couple of tornadoes. Anyone in the affected area should closely monitor forecasts and be prepared for rapidly changing weather conditions.#weather #severeweather #twister #wind

With many still cleaning up debris and working to restore power following a night of intense winds and storms, the Chicago area was again battered by severe weather Thursday.

Multiple confirmed tornadoes were reported in Streator and in Dwight, sparking warnings in LaSalle, Grundy and Kankakee counties.

A large and damaging tornado also made its way through Lake and Porter counties in Indiana, according to officials.

Friday should bring quieter conditions to the area, but clean-up efforts will once again have to begin in earnest in communities devastated by storms, and damage assessments will be underway to determine how many tornadoes struck the area.

authorMeta.name: runx2studio
text:
Having spent the past months producing AI short dramas with Seedance 2.0 + Fast, I’ve been able to observe the market dynamics firsthand—and the insights are fascinating:

* Rapid Production, Low Cost – A 2-minute episode can now be produced in roughly 10 days, using minimal compute (~200 seconds per episode). This efficiency challenges traditional production timelines and budgets.
* Market Imbalance – Globally, AI-driven short dramas are gaining traction. Yet in the UK, local production is almost non-existent. Platforms are largely unprepared for this wave, creating a clear opportunity for early movers.
* The Real Bottleneck: Storytelling – Technology is no longer the limiting factor. High-quality scripts remain scarce, and audience engagement depends heavily on narrative depth.
* Strategic Opportunity – For creators, studios, and platforms, the combination of human creativity + AI efficiency can unlock scalable, rapid, and cost-effective storytelling. Those who bridge the gap between strong narratives and AI production will define the next era of digital entertainment.

I’m actively exploring collaborations with writers, creative teams, and innovative platforms to push the boundaries of AI-generated storytelling. The future of short-form entertainment is hybrid—and it’s happening now.

#AI #ShortDrama #DigitalStorytelling #CreativeTech #ContentInnovation

UNESCO defines education as gaining knowledge, skills, values, and attitudes through formal, non-formal, and informal learning. It also describes education as lifelong, not limited to childhood or classrooms.  

So, are you educated? Yes—if you can learn, think, create, lead, question, solve, teach, build, and grow. You may not be “credentialed” as an educator, but you can still be educated by experience, discipline, research, observation, faith, culture, business, failure, and wisdom.

The real problem is that society often confuses certification with education. A certificate proves you completed a program. Education proves itself through what you can do with what you know.

For Africa, this discussion is serious. The World Bank says nearly 9 in 10 children in Sub-Saharan Africa cannot read and understand a simple text by age 10. UNESCO also reports that children taught in a familiar language are 30% more likely to read with understanding by the end of primary school.  

That means Africa does not only need more schools. Africa needs a new definition of education.

authorMeta.name: socratictranscript
text:
Education is not just about enrollment. It is about capability.
Dr. Shashi Tharoor said this about India — not the US — and it's worth listening to precisely because of that.
He's pointing out what we all feel here too: enrollment numbers are up, literacy rates look good on paper, more kids are "in school" than ever before. But being in a classroom doesn't mean you're being prepared for anything real.
Tharoor's argument is simple: India's system is producing degrees, not capabilities. Students graduate without the skills to get a job, without the ability to think critically about what they're told, and without the tools to actually participate in a democracy beyond just voting. It's rote memorization, outdated syllabi, and an obsession with marks over meaning.
Sound familiar?
That's because it's the exact same disease, just a different accent. The US system does it with AP overload and GPA worship. India does it with board exams and coaching centers. Different uniforms, same outcome: a generation that knows how to pass a test but freezes when life doesn't come with an answer key.
We know this isn't about the US — Tharoor is talking specifically about the Indian education system. But that's the point. Two completely different countries, two completely different cultures, and both have somehow perfected the art of making kids' lives miserable in the name of "education."
Both excel at taking curious 10-year-olds and turning them into anxious 17-year-olds who are great at filling in bubbles and terrible at figuring out who they are. Both confuse schooling with learning. Both measure success by how well you obey the system, not by whether you can actually do anything useful when you leave it.
Tharoor says we need to move beyond this — to build real cognitive ability, not just memory. Social ability, not just competition. Vocational ability, not just theoretical degrees. Because the purpose of education was never supposed to be a certificate to hang on a wall. It was supposed to be about shaping people who can think, work, question, and contribute.
India is waking up to it. We should too. Because whether you're in Mumbai prepping for JEE or in California prepping for the SAT, the problem is the same: the system isn't broken because it fails to enroll you. It's broken because it enrolls you and then leaves you unprepared.
{Shashi Tharoor, Education Reform, Skill Development, Indian Education System, Youth Empowerment, Critical Thinking, Future Of India, IIMUN}

authorMeta.name: smart.doctor
text:
See the Heart Valves 🩺🫀
.
.
The human heart is a vital organ that pumps blood throughout the body, supplying oxygen and nutrients while removing carbon dioxide and waste products. Here are some key points about the human heart:
Anatomy: The heart has four chambers: two upper atria and two lower ventricles. The right side pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs, while the left side pumps oxygenated blood to the rest of the body.
Valves: There are four main valves (tricuspid, pulmonary, mitral, and aortic) that ensure unidirectional blood flow through the heart and prevent backflow.
Circulation: The heart is part of the circulatory system, which includes the pulmonary circuit (to and from the lungs) and the systemic circuit (to and from the rest of the body).
Electrical System: The heart has its own electrical system, including the sinoatrial (SA) node, which acts as the natural pacemaker, regulating the heartbeat.
Health: Maintaining heart health is crucial, as cardiovascular diseases are leading causes of morbidity and mortality. Lifestyle factors such as diet, exercise, and avoiding smoking play a significant role in heart health.💓.
.
.

authorMeta.name: ned_grx
text:
🎨 Hobbies aren’t just fun — they’re health care. 🧩
Here’s how to implement hobbies for better wellness:
✅ Step 1 – Choose a low-pressure activity (drawing, gardening, playing an instrument, puzzles, dancing, etc.)
✅ Step 2 – Schedule just 15–20 minutes, 3x per week (start small to build consistency)
✅ Step 3 – Leave perfection behind — focus on enjoyment, not outcome
✅ Step 4 – Pair it with a habit (e.g., hobby time right after dinner or before bed)
Why it works:
🧠 Lowers stress & anxiety
😊 Boosts mood & creativity
🩶 May reduce risk of depression and even improve cognitive health
You don’t need to be “good” at your hobby. You just need to show up for yourself.
👇 Like if you have a hobby that makes you feel good
🔄 Share to inspire someone to start theirs
📲 Follow me for daily health tips
#HobbiesForHealth #WellnessTips #MentalWellbeing #DailyHealthTips

authorMeta.name: brain.fuel.daily8
text:
Master couple communications 😉 Most relationships don’t end because of lack of love.
They end because of misunderstandings, silence, ego, and poor communication.
A strong couple knows how to talk—even during difficult moments.
They don’t scream to hurt each other.
They don’t ignore each other for days.
They don’t turn every disagreement into a battle.
Instead, they listen.
They understand.
They communicate with respect, even when emotions are high.
Because real communication is not just talking—
it’s making your partner feel heard, valued, and safe enough to express themselves honestly.
A mature man communicates with patience, not anger.
A mature woman communicates with understanding, not manipulation.
That’s how healthy relationships grow.
Not by being perfect…
but by learning how to solve problems together instead of fighting against each other.
The strongest couples are the ones who say:
“Us against the problem, not me against you.”
Remember this—
love without communication creates confusion.
But love with honesty, effort, and emotional understanding
creates a bond that survives hard times.
So if you truly love someone,
learn to communicate before it’s too late. 🖤✨

COMMUNICATION

Most people think communication is about talking.

It isn’t, It’s about understanding.

You can say all the right words and still not feel heard. You can explain yourself perfectly and still be misunderstood.

The strongest relationships aren’t built on people who never disagree.

They’re built on people who are willing to listen, clarify and stay in the conversation when things get uncomfortable.

Communication isn’t just speaking.
It’s making sure what’s in your heart arrives where it was intended.

The Power of Showing Up First in Conversations............
Starting a conversation first is a simple act, but it carries strong psychological weight. It shows confidence, intention, and emotional availability.
People who take initiative are often more noticeable because they create connection instead of waiting for it. This consistency builds comfort, and comfort often leads to stronger bonds over time.
It’s not about chasing anyone — it’s about being present and willing to engage without fear.
In the end, small efforts in communication often create the biggest impact in relationships, friendships, and social connections.

Underrated aspect of this game: communication. 

Just becoming good at communicating on the field can avoid and solve many problems. This should be something that every center back should work on if they want to become elite. It keeps you focused and alert in the game, and it keeps the team organized. There is no such thing as bad communication on the field. A while ago I spoke about two different types of communication that every center back should aim at developing: charismatic and directional.

Charismatic: motivate your teammates to push themselves by being a leader and bringing a positive impact on the team.

Directional: moving the pieces of the puzzle around to fit the specific scenario that get created on the field.

Which one are you better at?

authorMeta.name: askvinh
text:
Here's the TOP 5 things you should do to improve your communication skills.

Great public speaking can seem really complex, but the truth is it's just a combination of small behaviors.

1/ Record and review yourself speaking - this helps build self-awareness on your vocal and visual image

2/ Match & mirror people's energy to build rapport and connection

3/ Learn how to pause (most people are afraid to pause because they're afraid of the silence and don't understand the true power of the pause!)

4/ Use the FORD method to never run out of things to say in conversations

5/ End your sentences on a low pitch. This the fastest hack to immediately come across with more certainty and authority when you communicate.

authorMeta.name: globalstatsx
text:
❤️ “I Love You” in 30 Different Languages

🌍 Language is one of the strongest bridges between cultures, and a simple phrase like “I love you” carries deep emotional meaning across the world. While the words may sound different, the feeling they express is universally understood and shared by people from every background.

🗣️ Each language reflects its own history, structure, and cultural nuances. Some expressions are short and direct, while others carry poetic or symbolic depth. Writing systems also vary widely, from Latin and Cyrillic alphabets to Arabic, Chinese characters, and other unique scripts, showing the richness of global linguistic diversity.

📚 Learning how common phrases are expressed in different languages helps improve cultural awareness and global understanding. It also highlights how communication adapts to different traditions while preserving the same core human emotions.

🌐 Multilingual expressions like these are often used in education, travel, cultural studies, and language learning. They remind us that despite geographical distance or linguistic differences, people around the world connect through shared values and emotions.

💬 Overall, this collection demonstrates how love is communicated universally, even though languages, alphabets, and pronunciations differ. It is a simple yet powerful example of how humanity remains connected through language.

#Languages #WorldLanguages #CulturalDiversity #GlobalCulture #LanguageLearning

authorMeta.name: unknow01351
text:
The Internet (or internet) is a global system of interconnected computer networks that use the standard Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP) to communicate between networks and devices. It is essentially a "network of networks" that consists of millions of private, public, academic, business, and government networks of local to global scope, linked by a broad array of electronic, wireless, and optical networking technologies. 
Key Aspects of the Internet:
Infrastructure: The Internet connects devices across buildings, cities, countries, and continents, primarily using physical infrastructure such as fiber optic cables, coaxial cables, and twisted pairs, along with wireless technology. 
Functionality: It operates as a vast, decentralized system for storing, transmitting, and exchanging information instantly, allowing for services like email, online chat, file transfers, and the World Wide Web (WWW). 
Protocol Suite (TCP/IP): This is the foundation of the Internet, providing the standard rules that allow diverse computers and systems to speak the same language and exchange data seamlessly. 
Purpose: The primary purpose of the Internet is to connect people and devices, acting as the largest source of information and an interactive platform for education, commerce, and communication. 
In essence, it is the most creative and useful interactive innovation, providing access to, and exchange of, information across the globe, effectively making the world a smaller place.

authorMeta.name: xchlo.r
text:
dead internet theory.

#deadinternettheroy #deadinternet #bots

The Dead Internet Theory is a conspiracy theory that claims most of the internet is no longer controlled by real people. According to this theory, a large amount of online content is created by bots, artificial intelligence, and automated systems instead of humans. Supporters of the theory believe that this change began sometime around the mid-2010s and has continued to grow over time.

People who believe in the Dead Internet Theory point to several observations. They argue that many social media posts, comments, and websites seem repetitive or artificial. They also note that automated accounts, known as bots, are common on platforms such as social media websites. These bots can post messages, share content, and even interact with users without human involvement.

Although the theory raises interesting questions, there is little evidence to support the claim that most of the internet is "dead." Experts agree that bots and AI-generated content exist and have become more common, but millions of real people still use the internet every day to communicate, learn, work, and share their experiences. The growth of artificial intelligence has made it harder to tell whether some content was created by a human or a machine, which may contribute to the popularity of the theory.

The Dead Internet Theory is important because it encourages people to think critically about the information they see online. As technology continues to advance, users should be aware that not everything on the internet comes from a real person. Checking sources, verifying information, and using reliable websites are important skills in the digital age.

In conclusion, the Dead Internet Theory is an interesting idea that suggests much of the internet is controlled by bots and artificial intelligence. While there is evidence that automated content exists, there is no proof that the internet is mostly made up of non-human activity. The theory remains a popular topic of discussion because it reflects growing concerns about technology, AI, and the future of online communication.

authorMeta.name: oneyoungworld
text:
Up to 200 wildlife species go extinct every single day. 🚨

For our Ambassador Simón Torras, that isn't just a headline. 

Watching his home country Mexico, lose up to 200,000 hectares of forest every year, he decided to take action.

He founded Ponterra to restore deforested land by partnering with local farmers to replant native trees — rebuilding ecosystems while empowering the communities that depend on them.

And in Panama’s Azuero Peninsula, the impact has been huge:

🌱 10,000+ hectares restored
🌳 75+ native tree species planted
🌍 4 million+ tonnes of CO2 captured
🤝 300+ local jobs created
💚 $70 million+ in community benefits generated

Simón is showing the world what climate leadership looks like. Because nature needs more than awareness, it needs action.

Repost this video and be part of the comeback. 💚

#OneYoungWorld #Reforestation #ClimateChange #Mexico

authorMeta.name: goinggreenmedia
text:
CLEANING RIVERS WITH SOLAR ENERGY💧🌞

Follow us for more projects like this! 

We recently visited this project on our trip to South Korea and were blown away by just how much waste they can collect.

We were also pleased to find out they are animal friendly, as they are very slow moving and have guards to protect ducks and birds 🦆 

Using solar power, the ECOBOT made by Ecopeace can clean all day, and can target the most polluted, hard to reach areas by following a route set by water management operators.

It removes surface waste, and helps with algae blooms, and it can even clean up oil spills using microfilters and electrochemical treatment.

It also has the potential to transport people autonomously, while simultaneously acting as a waste and climate mitigation device.

By collecting data in real-world conditions and environments, they can help operators create a better understanding of even remote bodies of water.

It’s a great preventive tool to help cities understand their water pollution before things get worse. 

They now operate across South Korea, and southeast Asia–but they need more exposure to go worldwide.

authorMeta.name: anna..papalia
text:
Here are 28 great questions to ask in a job interview 
@Anna Papalia 

Questions about the specific job
1.     What are your expectations for me in this role?
2.    What’s the most important thing I should accomplish in the first 90 days (https://hbr.org/books/watkins)?
3.    What’s the performance review process like here? How often would I be formally reviewed?
4.    What metrics or goals will my performance be evaluated against?
5.    What are the most immediate projects that I would take on?
6.    How long before I will be… [meeting with clients, have responsibility for my own accounts, interacting with other departments, etc.]?

Questions about the team
7.    What types of skills is the team missing that you’re looking to fill with a new hire?
8.   What are the biggest challenges that I might face in this position?
9.    Do you expect my main responsibilities in this position to change in the next six months to a year?
10.Can you tell me about the team I’ll be working with?
11.  Who will I work with most closely? What other departments or units will I interact with?
12. Can you tell me about my direct reports? What are their strengths and the team’s biggest challenges?

Questions for your potential boss
13. How long have you been at the company?
14. How long have you been a manager?
15. What’s your favorite part of working here?
Questions about the company 
16. What are the current goals that the company is focused on, and how does this team work to support hitting those goals?
17. What gets you most excited about the company’s future?
18.How would you describe the company’s values?
19. How has the company changed over the last few years?
20.                 What are the company’s plans for growth and development?

Questions about the culture
21. How do you typically onboard employees? 
o   If the position will be remote, ask specifically about how remote employees are integrated into the company culture, Markman advises.
22.                  What do new employees typically find surprising after they start?
23.                  Is there anything that I should read before starting that would help me have a shared understanding with my colleagues? 
o   Asking this question not only signals your interest in the position but also shows that you’re eager to have “shared cultural references with the people you’ll be working with,” Markman says.
24.                  What’s your favorite office tradition?
25.                  What do you and the team usually do for lunch?
26.                  Do you ever do joint events with other departments or teams?
27.What’s different about working here than anywhere else you’ve worked?
28.                  How has the co
mpany changed since you joined?

#jobinterview #interviewprep #interviewtips #questionstoask #howtointerview

authorMeta.name: anna..papalia
text:
😱Never accept a job offer without negotiating.  When you receive a job offer always say, “Thank you so much, is this negotiable?” 

👉Watch @Anna Papalia Things I Don’t Want to See on Your Resume👈

❌You cannot accept a job offer and then try to negotiate because you don’t have any leverage. 

✅You have to negotiate your job offer before you accept. 

❌Do not go into a negotiation not knowing what you want and what you can ask for. 

👩🏼‍💻When they say they can’t increase your salary, you can negotiate other things: 

🔹Sign on bonus
🔹More PTO
🔹Better title
🔹Work from home/flexible schedule
🔹Tuition reimbursement 

👉Watch @Anna Papalia How to Answer, What Are Your Salary Expectations?”👈

👉Watch @Anna Papalia Things I Don’t Want to See in a Job Interview 👈

✅When you ask for what you want, state it plainly. 

❌Do not overexplain, do not say that you need more money because you have 4 kids in college” They don’t care. Their job is to figure out what you want and see if they can make it happen. 

✅It’s business not personal. 

✅Silence is power in a negotiation. 

⚠️If you try to leverage an existing offer to get more money you better believe they will ask to see your offer letter,  so it better be a real job offer. 

#salarynegotiation #joboffer #howto #jobsearch #jobinterview
